records.authority: National Institute of Standards and Technology, Applied and Computational Mathematics Division
records.excerpt: The SLATEC Library is a public-domain Fortran library.
records.statement: NIST expressly describes the SLATEC Library, Version 4.1, as public domain and identifies Netlib as its whole-or-part distribution channel.
records.scope: SLATEC Common Mathematical Library, Version 4.1 (July 1993); not an inference merely from NIST association.

records.authority: National Technical Information Service, U.S. Department of Commerce
records.excerpt: SLATEC is a public domain library of over 1000 mathematical routines.
records.statement: The authenticated NTIS record independently describes the SLATEC routine library as public domain and names included packages including LINPACK, EISPACK, DEPAC, FISHPACK, and QUADPACK.
records.scope: SLATEC routines described by the 1987 SLADOC technical report; corroborating institutional evidence, not a licence inferred from sponsorship.

records.excerpt: FNLIB subset of SLATEC; main SLATEC Fortran source.
records.statement: The Version 4.1 Netlib index identifies the selected fnlib and src units as subsets of the SLATEC Common Mathematical Library, rather than unrelated Netlib-hosted code.
records.scope: Only the selected source URLs under https://www.netlib.org/slatec/fnlib/ and https://www.netlib.org/slatec/src/.

records.authority: NIST and NTIS public-domain statements, scoped to Netlib SLATEC Version 4.1 source identities
records.excerpt: The SLATEC Library is a public-domain Fortran library; FNLIB subset of SLATEC; main SLATEC Fortran source.
records.statement: This release-review record combines NIST's express SLATEC public-domain statement and the independent NTIS SLADOC public-domain record with Netlib's Version 4.1 fnlib/src subset identification. It applies only to the exact, hash-pinned source set below; it is not a directory-wide inference and it does not clear other Netlib-hosted material.
records.scope: The exact source closures of the listed scalar special-function and scalar-root families, including the real Bessel and scalar-expanded closures, identified by the deterministic source-set SHA-256 and count.
